title: Stable Diffusion Cheat Sheet
description: A practical guide for engineers using Stable Diffusion for image generation.

commands.description: Generates an image based on the given text prompt.
commands.scenario: When you need to create concept art for a sci-fi project.

commands.description: Sets the number of diffusion steps for image generation.
commands.scenario: When you want a higher quality image with more detail.

commands.description: Adjusts the classifier-free guidance scale for image generation.
commands.scenario: When you need to balance creativity and adherence to the prompt.

commands.description: Sets a specific seed for reproducible image generation.
commands.scenario: When you need consistent outputs for testing purposes.

commands.description: Specifies the directory to save generated images.
commands.scenario: When organizing output files for a project.

commands.description: Sets the resolution of the generated image.
commands.scenario: When you need a specific image size for a publication.

commands.description: Specifies the path to the model to be used for generation.
commands.scenario: When using a custom or specific version of the model.

commands.description: Generates a specified number of images from a single prompt.
commands.scenario: When you need multiple variations of an image concept.

commands.description: Enables half-precision floating-point for faster generation.
commands.scenario: When optimizing for speed and resource usage.

commands.command: "Imagine a world where AI and humans coexist peacefully."
commands.description: A creative prompt to inspire peaceful coexistence themed images.
commands.scenario: When creating artwork for a campaign promoting AI ethics.

commands.description: Sets the noise schedule parameter for the diffusion process.
commands.scenario: When fine-tuning the noise schedule for specific artistic effects.

commands.description: Streams the image generation process in real-time.
commands.scenario: When demonstrating the generation process in a live setting.

commands.description: Defines tokens to explicitly exclude from the generation process to improve image fidelity.
Specifies the mathematical sampling algorithm used to denoise the latent space (e.g., Euler a, DPM++ 2M Karras).
Loads a specific Variational Autoencoder to correct color banding and improve fine detail reconstruction.
Enables memory-efficient attention mechanisms to significantly reduce VRAM usage and increase generation speed.
Skips a specific number of layers in the CLIP text encoder to allow for more creative/abstract interpretations of prompts.
